title: Model cross-restart download resume + partial detection + close-app warning
description: Models (safetensors up to 20GB) must resume after a full app close, not restart. node-downloader-helper writes to the FINAL filename with no .part, so a killed partial reads as installed=true (models/check uses bare fs.pathExists -> truncated model loaded). Plan: (1) sidecar marker <file>.cubricdl written on download start, deleted on verified complete; central isCompleteOnDisk(path) = exists && no sidecar, used by routes/comfy.js models/check + all install 'isInstalled' checks + UW deps checks. (2) ResumableDownloader.resumeFromDisk(): on a fresh instance, if partial+sidecar exist, manual resumeFromFile() (verified to continue AND keep pause working) instead of start(). Wire into model download start path. (3) pause() crash safety: defer abort via setImmediate (ERR_STREAM_WRITE_AFTER_END mid-chunk). (4) Both-tier close-app warning: Electron before-quit queries new GET /comfy/downloads/active {models:[],engine:bool}; models in flight -> 'will resume next launch', engine -> 'will restart'. Engine stays restart-from-scratch (already handled). DO NOT use resumeIfFileExists/override on the constructor (breaks in-session pause). See memory project_ndh_resumable_downloads + project_engine_bootstrap_retry. Verify on real desktop: kill mid model download, relaunch, confirm resume + correct installed state + working pause.
